module_mood_contract: concrete awe — 'this is not magic; it is structure'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured per beat.
Strategy-fidelity: no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:35 — Handoff: 'individual signal first, group synthesis second, cross-pollination only after synthesis.' Strategy doc (§ Agentic systems, shown in the repo → Bridge + § Where is this all going? → Teaching moment) states the ordering is what the room DISCOVERS by doing M2, then organisers NAME it after: 'Nobody taught it as architecture; the work needed that ordering, so you used it.' Naming it in M1's Handoff turns a felt discovery into a pre-announced procedure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Strip the three-part ordering from the Handoff; leave only the horizon: 'Now that you have seen the shape of a system, run one together.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands (per phase + close): mood_score 8+ required at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score: 8 (teaching beat — mood-floor applies). trace.close.mood_score: 7 (facilitator-premium; close flatness attributable to the Handoff pre-announcement, same root as strategy-fidelity finding above).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Fixing the Handoff (strategy-fidelity finding) will likely recover the close to 8+ by removing the pre-announced ordering.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early: M1 must not preempt M2's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].confusion_flags[1] and trace.ambiguous_instructions[0]: Handoff line names the M2 diverge→synthesize→cross-pollinate ordering as a pre-instruction. M2's strategy teaching moment is the room realising AFTER the fact that they ran that ordering because the work needed it. Naming it before M2 steals the discovery arc.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Same fix as strategy-fidelity: remove the three-part ordering clause from the Handoff. The bridge can name the activity ('run one together') without naming the architecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands: persona reliably reaches the 'Claude is not only the chat box' insight.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0]: Opening Question creates the 'chat box' gap. What to Notice bullets (CLAUDE.md, prompts, evals, build output, loop) are the lens for the demo. The demo script (agentic-systems-demo-script.md) carries the live walk; the module body sets up the persona to receive it. Teaching moment is structurally robust across reasonable persona-skill variation because the five bullets pre-organise the demo's signal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: file leads with the strategy doc's Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File Big Idea ('Agentic systems become visible when you know where to look: rules, prompts, memory, checks, build scripts, customer versions, and commits') matches strategy doc § Agentic systems, shown in the repo Big Idea verbatim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: M1 close hands off to M2 open cleanly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:35 — Handoff names M2's discovery pattern ('individual signal first, group synthesis second, cross-pollination only after synthesis') before M2 runs. Strategy doc § Arc Logic M2: 'The training accidentally shows the team the ordering they need.' That accidental quality is designed — it requires NOT pre-naming the ordering in M1. A correct handoff from M1 invites the work without revealing its architecture.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Handoff should create forward pull without narrating M2's structure. Candidate: 'Now that you have seen the shape of a system, run one together.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'compare with the person next to you', or equivalent cohort-management prose found in student-facing body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module has no fenced prompt blocks — it is a trainer-led demo module. Rule does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement: demo lectures showing a ceiling go at the beginning.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The agentic-systems-demo-script.md lecture is placed at module open, correctly positioned as a ceiling-showing demo (check_lectures.md rule 1: 'Demo lectures showing a ceiling go at the beginning').
No shame/failure framing present. Module creates curiosity about structure, not anxiety about past mistakes.

stealing_the_mood: Close at 7 (facilitator-premium, not blocking on its own): Handoff pre-announces M2's diverge→synthesize→cross-pollinate ordering, flattening the forward hunger the close should carry. Same root cause as the blocking strategy-fidelity finding.
